[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Callback payer confusion where tokens are pulled from the wrong party or an explicit payer can be bypassed'] [Function: take / receiver equals payer] For a buy offer with callback, payer = offer.callback and receiver = receiverIfTakerIsSeller. If receiver == payer (offer.callback == receiverIfTakerIsSeller), Midnight calls safeTransferFrom(token, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) and safeTransferFrom(token, payer, payer, sellerAssets). The second call is a self-transfer. Can this cause accounting issues or allow payer to net out sellerAssets from their payment obligation? Can a taker exploit this to force the maker to pay buyerAssets while the taker receives sellerAssets, effectively getting a free loan?
